title: Compositional Reservoir Simulation
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is a widefield-fluorescence fan-out artifact; principle grounded in domain literature.

E.description: Compositional Reservoir Simulation: Compositional simulation: track N_c component flow with EOS phase equilibrium calculations for gas condensate and miscible floods. The forward operator produces the measurement through a 3-node primitive DAG (M.eos.peng_robinson…); recovery is posed as a parameter_estimation problem. Difficulty tier delta=5 with effective condition number kappa_eff~5000; EOS_tuning_uncertainty, compositional_gradient_uncertainty set the accuracy floor at the Omega boundary. See the forward_model field for the closed-form equation.
E.forward_model: phi*(d(rho*z_i)/dt) = -nabla*(rho_o*x_i*u_o + rho_g*y_i*u_g) + q_i; K_i = y_i/x_i from EOS
E.world_state_x: component mole fractions z_i(x,t) and pressure P(x,t) across 3D grid
E.observation_y: wellhead compositional analysis, GOR, condensate-gas ratio, BHP

W.regime: Existence of the recovered 3D_composition_pressure is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness holds on the measurement-supported subspace; out-of-support modes are controlled by declared priors. Stability is conditionally stable (kappa_eff ~= 5000); EOS_tuning_uncertainty dominates the stability cliff; the remaining mismatch parameters contribute higher-order bias terms. Measurement gaussian sets the irreducible data-fidelity floor.

C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: GOR prediction < 10% error for tuned EOS. Noise floor: O(sigma / sqrt(kappa_eff)) with kappa_eff~5000. Mismatch-induced bias + C2 * |EOS_tuning_uncertainty|; iterative solvers achieve q=2 convergence (O(1/k^2)) near the optimum; epsilon_fn captures quality degradation across declared Omega.
C.complexity: O(N_cells * N_c ** 2 * N_Newton * N_timesteps) for flash calculations per iteration
